mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a high threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy that increases the discount rate for corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest FEDFUNDS value is 5.33, indicating a restrictive policy stance.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a 10-year lookback.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls is a direct indicator of a robust and expanding economy. This strength translates into higher expected corporate earnings and consumer demand, driving positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent PAYEMS releases show continued job additions.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 1-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a tight labor market (low unemployment) and elevated inflation creates a 'stagflationary' pressure that forces the central bank to maintain or increase policy rates, negatively impacting equity valuations due to higher discount rates and recession fears.
evidence_refs: fact:Current conditions (UNRATE=3.8, high CPI) meet the trigger.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime, stronger-than-expected real GDP growth is interpreted negatively by markets, as it signals persistent inflationary pressure that will necessitate further central bank hawkishness, thus depressing equity prices.
failure_condition: When the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is positive, triggered instances of high GDPC1 growth are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating trend in industrial production indicates strengthening economic output and corporate activity. This 'hard' data signal leads to upward revisions in earnings forecasts and drives positive short-term equity market performance.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, signaled by a significant increase in the unemployment rate from its recent low, is a reliable leading indicator of recession. This triggers a flight to safety and a sell-off in equities.
candidate_trigger: 3-month avg of UNRATE rises > 0.5pp from its 12-month low
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following a trigger, the S&P 500 median return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When nominal retail sales growth is outpaced by consumer price inflation, it signifies a contraction in real consumer spending. This erosion of real demand is a leading indicator of weakening corporate revenues and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 12-month % change in RSAFS < 12-month % change in CPIAUCSL
failure_condition: The hit rate (correctly predicting negative returns) of the signal is below 55% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts is a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, as residential investment is a key component of GDP and is highly sensitive to interest rates. This weakness signals lower future growth and negatively impacts equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector is known to be interest-rate sensitive.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive on average following a trigger event over the last three business cycles.
